_doc: Registry of auditable name sources used or planned for P2/P2b. Only include sources that can be cited and traced.

sources.citation_short: Bertrand & Mullainathan (2004)-inspired audit-name subset, manually curated in this repository
sources.notes: Current repository source. Suitable for mechanism experiments, but too small and too sparse for a definitive tokenizer/rarity decomposition.

sources.citation_short: Bertrand, M., and S. Mullainathan. 2003. NBER Working Paper w9873. DOI: 10.3386/w9873
sources.notes: Verified from the NBER paper page in this session. This is a citable reference for classic audit names, but not by itself a large database.

sources.citation_short: To be filled only after the official frequency dataset URL/license/citation are verified
sources.notes: Placeholder for the next verified database-backed expansion. In this session, one candidate official source page returned access denied, so it remains unverified.

sources.citation_short: Rosenman, E. T. R., S. Olivella, and K. Imai. 2023. Race and ethnicity data for first, middle, and last names. Harvard Dataverse. DOI: 10.7910/DVN/SGKW0K
sources.notes: Chosen as the primary external expansion source for the next P2b rebuild. It is larger and more directly aligned with race-labeled first-name coverage than names-dataset, while remaining citable and public. Caveat: the probabilities come from six Southern-state voter files, so any final writeup should note potential population-shift limitations. In this repository it is now used as the main external source for the expanded P2b matrix and targeted validation.

sources.citation_short: philipperemy/name-dataset, version 3.3.1, Apache-2.0; country/gender/popularity-rank metadata from a Facebook-derived name database
sources.notes: Kept only as a historical auxiliary validation source. It was useful for race-agnostic tokenization/popularity checks, but it is no longer the preferred external database for the main P2b expansion.
